completed_steps.title: The Witness Fails to Appear
completed_steps.summary: Arthur Penhaligon, waiting in the hallway for the private midnight revelation Sir Alistair promised, grows concerned by the silence and enters the study to find the industrialist slumped over his desk.
reveals: Sir Alistair Thorne is dead.

completed_steps.title: The Household Gathers
completed_steps.summary: Arthur informs the guests of the tragedy. He notes the reactions: Julian's sweating, Elspeth's icy detachment, and the Vances' clinical shock.
reveals: Julian Thorne was wearing a green costume that is now missing a ribbon.

completed_steps.title: The Silent Library
completed_steps.summary: Arthur checks Lady Elspeth's claim that she was reading in the library. He finds her wine glass with distinct lipstick marks, but the book she claimed to be reading is dust-covered and hasn't been moved in weeks.
reveals: Lady Elspeth's alibi is fabricated; she was not reading.

completed_steps.title: The Weapon in the Ashes
completed_steps.summary: A search of the study's hearth reveals a hunting knife wiped clean, but hidden hastily beneath the logs.
reveals: A decorative hunting knife was used to stab the victim.

completed_steps.title: The Heir's Panic
completed_steps.summary: Arthur confronts Julian about the green ribbon (EV-02) and the knife. Julian becomes aggressive and defensive, claiming he was on the terrace alone.
reveals: Julian Thorne has no witnesses for his whereabouts between 10:00 PM and 10:45 PM.

remaining_steps.title: The Muddy Terrace
remaining_steps.summary: Arthur examines the terrace. Despite Julian's claim of clearing his head, there are no footprints in the mud near the door, and the storm would have soaked anyone standing there in seconds.
reveals: Julian Thorne's alibi is physically impossible given the weather.

remaining_steps.title: The Motive of Debt
remaining_steps.summary: Arthur discovers a hidden ledger page in Julian's drawer. It details massive gambling debts that would provide a clear motive for murder to secure an immediate inheritance.
reveals: Julian Thorne was being hunted by debt collectors and needed his father's money urgently.

remaining_steps.title: The Doctor's Deception
remaining_steps.summary: As Arthur questions Dr. Vance, he notices the doctor attempting to shove a piece of paper into his medical bag. Arthur insists on seeing it, revealing a blackmail note.
reveals: Dr. Vance was being blackmailed by Alistair over a fatal surgical error.

remaining_steps.title: The Loyal Wife
remaining_steps.summary: Arthur speaks with Eleanor Vance. She is calm and supportive of her husband, insisting Percival was with her or in the washroom all evening, though her mask adjustments were conveniently unobserved.
reveals: Eleanor Vance was remarkably attentive to her husband's deteriorating mental state.

remaining_steps.title: The Gardener's Secret
remaining_steps.summary: Suspecting the poison's origin, Arthur visits the conservatory. He finds a discarded suede glove in the bin with crushed organic residue and soil.
reveals: Someone recently harvested monkshood from the manor's collection.

remaining_steps.title: The Beaded Handbag
remaining_steps.summary: While the guests are resting, Arthur checks the cloaks and bags in the hall. Inside Eleanor Vance's handbag, he finds a single, tell-tale purple petal.
reveals: Eleanor Vance was in possession of monkshood petals during the masquerade.

remaining_steps.title: A Bitter Vintage
remaining_steps.summary: Eleanor Vance confesses, stating she would do it again to stop Alistair's cruelty from killing her husband's spirit. As the storm breaks and the police arrive at dawn, she is taken into custody while Julian and Elspeth are left to face the shame of their own smaller crimes.
reveals: The case is closed; the motive was protection, not greed.

remaining_steps.title: The Staged Demise
remaining_steps.summary: Arthur Penhaligon secures the study, noting Sir Alistair's body. He finds the prominent decorative hunting knife (EV-08) near the victim, but its wound doesn't appear to be the immediate cause of death. He also discovers a torn green silk ribbon (EV-02) snagged on the desk and a blackmail letter (EV-04) addressed to 'P' tucked into a ledger.
reveals: The stab wound appears post-mortem, suggesting an attempt to obscure the true cause of death.
Sir Alistair was blackmailing someone identified as 'P'.
There may have been a struggle or hurried departure by someone wearing a masquerade costume.

remaining_steps.title: A Secret Revealed
remaining_steps.summary: Following the discovery of the blackmail letter (EV-04), Arthur Penhaligon confronts Dr. Percival Vance in the library. Initially evasive, Percival confesses Alistair was blackmailing him over a past medical mistake that threatened to ruin his career and reputation. He reveals his wife, Eleanor, was deeply distressed by the situation and vowed to protect him.
reveals: Dr. Percival Vance had a strong motive for Alistair's death.
Eleanor Vance was aware of the blackmail and deeply troubled by it, providing her with a potential motive to protect her husband.

remaining_steps.title: Botanical Betrayal
remaining_steps.summary: Prompted by Percival's confession and the general search of the estate, Arthur Penhaligon visits the conservatory, known for its exotic and medicinal plants. He notices a disturbed monkshood plant and discovers a stained suede gardening glove (EV-03) tucked away near a potting bench, bearing traces of crushed plant matter and dirt.
reveals: Someone recently handled the poisonous monkshood plant.
The gardening glove could be linked to the preparation of a toxin.

remaining_steps.title: Hidden in Plain Sight
remaining_steps.summary: Following up on the discovery of the gardening glove (EV-03) and the disturbed monkshood in the conservatory, Arthur Penhaligon conducts a discreet search of Eleanor Vance's personal effects in her sitting room. Inside her beaded evening handbag, he finds a small, dried monkshood petal (EV-07), suggesting a direct connection between Eleanor and the poisonous plant.
reveals: Eleanor Vance has directly handled monkshood, the source of Aconitine poison.
This strengthens the link between Eleanor and the method of poisoning, especially when combined with the stained glove and her motive to protect Percival.
